mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean direction in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates robust manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of periods when INDPRO increases, or the signal fails to predict direction during documented recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation readings above 7% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered months, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for prior rate changes in a regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly payroll growth exceeding 200,000 and a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening and wage growth, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2% annualized reflects sustained economic expansion and corporate profit momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no directional bias or are negative in >60% of quarters when GDPC1 exceeds the 2% threshold, or rolling predictive accuracy falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production increase exceeding 0.5% signals robust manufacturing demand and capacity utilization, reducing recession fears and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power during documented expansion periods in walk-forward validation.
